dimensions.label: Scope
dimensions.reason: In/out boundaries are crisp: no FB/IG/X friend import, no replay/spectate, no real-time multiplayer, no paid backend tier.

dimensions.label: Risk
dimensions.reason: Main risk is bit-identical Dart->TypeScript PRNG/seeder parity for server-side replay verification; secondary risk is contacts-permission store review.

dimensions.label: Effort
dimensions.reason: Sizable but well-understood; the only genuinely new surface is the Supabase backend and edge function.

dimensions.label: Clarity
dimensions.reason: Every major decision (tiers, identity, daily window, anti-cheat, friend channels, engagement set) is resolved.

dimensions.label: Tests
dimensions.reason: The deterministic engine is highly testable; a cross-language equivalence test (same seed -> same sequence) pins the riskiest part.

problem: merge_loop is a polished but solitary single-player daily puzzle: every player gets one deterministic board per day (seeded from a SHA256 of the date) and plays it alone. There is no reason to return beyond personal best, no social pull, and no way to compare your run against anyone else's. Engagement, reach, and session count per day are all left on the table.

Separately, the AdMob configuration is nearly correct but has a release-blocking defect: the real ad unit IDs are wired up and useTestAds is false, yet the iOS GADApplicationIdentifier in Info.plist still holds Google's TEST App ID. On iOS this mismatch (real unit IDs + test App ID) prevents ads from serving and can get the app flagged.

The goal is to convert the solo daily puzzle into a competitive, social, habit-forming daily game - difficulty tiers, global and friends leaderboards, identity, and retention mechanics - while keeping infrastructure cost at $0 (Supabase free tier) and shipping a cheat-resistant leaderboard from day one.
goals: Fix the iOS AdMob App ID so ads serve correctly on both platforms.
Add 4 difficulty tiers (easy=10, medium=8, hard=6, legendary=4 starting tiles; 30 moves all tiers), each a separate deterministic daily board playable once per tier per day.
Seed boards and leaderboards by UTC date so 'everyone plays the same board today' holds globally.
Ship a global per-tier daily leaderboard with server-side replay-verified scores (cheating effectively impossible).
Give every player an identity via Supabase anonymous auth + display name with zero signup friction.
Add a friends/local leaderboard built from friend codes + contacts matching, plus outbound score sharing to any platform.
Add retention mechanics: streaks + local daily-reminder notifications, achievements/badges, extended (weekly/monthly/all-time) leaderboards, and a practice mode.
Keep backend cost at $0 on Supabase free tier; design for a clean upgrade path as player count grows.
successCriteria: On a real iOS build, real ads serve and Info.plist GADApplicationIdentifier is the real App ID (not ...3940256099942544).
Selecting each of the 4 tiers loads a distinct, deterministic board; the same tier on the same UTC date yields an identical board for every player; tile counts are 10/8/6/4.
A player can complete each tier exactly once per UTC day; a second attempt that day is blocked.

Submitting a completed run posts the move sequence; the Supabase edge function replays it against the regenerated (date,tier) board and writes a score that matches the client's locally computed score for legitimate runs.
A fabricated or illegal move sequence is rejected by the edge function and never reaches the leaderboard.
The global daily leaderboard for a given tier shows ranked display names + scores and resets at 00:00 UTC.
A player can generate a friend code/link, a second device can redeem it, and both appear on each other's friends leaderboard.
Contacts matching (with permission granted) surfaces at least known opted-in test contacts; denying permission degrades gracefully.
A local notification fires at the configured daily time and when a streak is about to expire.
Daily streak increments on consecutive-day completion and resets after a missed UTC day; achievements unlock on their trigger conditions.
Cross-language test: the TypeScript PRNG and seeder produce byte-identical output to the Dart implementations for a battery of seeds.
flutter analyze is clean and flutter test passes, including new engine/seeder/tier tests.

mvp.item: Fix iOS AdMob App ID in Info.plist
mvp.reason: Release-blocking defect; one-line fix, no reason to defer.

mvp.item: UTC-date seeding (replace local-date) with local countdown to reset
mvp.reason: Prerequisite for a fair global 'same board for all' leaderboard.

mvp.item: 4 difficulty tiers via per-tier starting fill (10/8/6/4), 30 moves all tiers
mvp.reason: Core game expansion; tile-count-only keeps it deterministic and balanceable.

mvp.item: Per-(date,tier) seeding, snapshots, and stats; once-per-tier-per-day rule
mvp.reason: Each tier is its own daily puzzle and its own competition.

mvp.item: Record the move sequence per run
mvp.reason: Required input for server-side replay verification; cheap to add now.
Foundation for all online features at $0.

mvp.item: Anonymous auth + display name (with optional avatar/emoji)
mvp.reason: Zero-friction identity maximizes leaderboard participation and ad views.

mvp.item: Server-side replay verification (Dart engine/PRNG/seeder ported to a TS edge function)
mvp.reason: A money-tied public leaderboard must be cheat-resistant from day one; determinism makes it feasible.

full.reason: Primary, permission-free path to a friends leaderboard; inherently viral.
Classic 'find friends who play'; needs only the contacts permission.
Realistic growth lever since friend-import is impossible on IG/X; reuses score_share_screen.
Ranks your connected friends by today's score.

full.item: Streak system surfacing + milestones + rewarded-ad streak freeze
full.reason: #1 retention lever; streak is already tracked but unused.
Biggest DAU driver and $0 (no backend push needed).
Goals beyond the single day; simple Supabase aggregation.

stretch.item: Cosmetic tile themes (unlock via streaks/achievements or rewarded ad)

outOfScope.reason: Requires Meta App Review and only returns mutual app users; deferred to keep scope and review burden down.
No supported friend-graph API on Instagram; X follower endpoints are paid-only. Replaced by outbound sharing.
Nice-to-have (and near-free given recorded moves) but explicitly cut from this round.
This is async daily competition by design, not live PvP.

outOfScope.item: Paid Supabase tier / managed push (FCM) infrastructure
outOfScope.reason: Stay at $0 until player count justifies an upgrade.
Anonymous-first; account linking is a future enhancement.

future: Replay & spectate the day's top runs (architecture already records move sequences)
Facebook login + friends import once an audience justifies Meta review
Account linking for cross-device identity recovery
Push notifications when a friend beats your score (needs FCM)
Seasons with resets and seasonal badges

phases.title: Ads fix + Difficulty tiers + UTC seeding
phases.notes: Client-only, no backend. Fix iOS App ID; switch seeding to UTC; add 4 tiers via per-tier starting fill; key snapshots/stats by (date,tier); enforce once-per-tier-per-day; record the move sequence per run; tier-select UI + local reset countdown.

phases.title: Supabase backend + identity + replay-verified global leaderboard
prereqs: Ads fix + Difficulty tiers + UTC seeding
phases.notes: Supabase project, schema + RLS, anonymous auth + display name. Port prng.dart + daily_seeder.dart + the merge engine to a TS edge function; verify runs by replay; write authoritative scores. Global per-tier daily leaderboard UI. Highest-risk task is bit-identical cross-language PRNG parity.

phases.title: Friends / local leaderboard
prereqs: Supabase backend + identity + replay-verified global leaderboard
phases.notes: Friend codes + invite deep links; contacts permission + hashed matching; outbound share cards; friends leaderboard UI. Parallelizable with Phase 4.

phases.title: Retention & engagement
prereqs: Supabase backend + identity + replay-verified global leaderboard
phases.notes: Streak surfacing + milestones + rewarded streak-freeze; local daily/streak notifications; achievements/badges; extended (weekly/monthly/all-time) leaderboards; practice mode + extra rewarded-ad spots (stretch). Client-only parts parallelize with Phase 3.

execution.agentTeamPrompt: After Phase 2 is merged, two independent tracks can run in parallel. Teammate A: implement Phase 3 (Friends / local leaderboard) from docs/ideation/competitive-daily-expansion/spec-phase-3.md. Teammate B: implement Phase 4 (Retention & engagement) from docs/ideation/competitive-daily-expansion/spec-phase-4.md. Both depend only on Phase 2 (identity + backend), not on each other. Coordinate on shared files (lib/main.dart, pubspec.yaml, the Supabase schema/migrations, and any shared leaderboard widgets) to avoid merge conflicts - only one teammate should modify a shared file at a time.
